Strengths: Handles sparse single-cell data; missing modalities; interpretable latent factors
Limitations: Not intrinsically count-model based; linear factor assumption
Best_for: Single-cell multi-omics integration; cross-modal alignment; cell-state discovery
Output_formats: Latent factors, feature loadings, variance explained, factor associations

Use_case: Unsupervised integration of samples in cross-condition single-cell atlases, latent factor discovery
Strengths: Handles missing data; flexible view definition with non-overlapping features; joint modeling of independent cohorts
Limitations: Dependency on pseudobulk profiles of cell types; insufficient nuclei count per sample for certain cell types; linear latent space
Best_for: Identification of multicellular programs associated with covariates in large single-cell atlases
Preprocessing_steps: pseudobulk count generation and normalization, feature selection
Input_details: Paired pseudobulk samples across cell types
Output_formats: Latent factors, feature loadings, variance explained, factor associations
Underlying_method: MOFA applied to pseudobulk expression matrices across cell types to extract multicellular transcriptional signature

Use_case: scRNA-seq + scATAC-seq integration; multimodal cell-state discovery; atlas construction
Strengths: Widely adopted; easy integration into Seurat workflows; interpretable modality weights; excellent visualization tools; supports multimodal neighbors and clustering; large user community
Limitations: Not a fully generative model; no explicit uncertainty quantification; memory-intensive for large datasets; limited probabilistic interpretation; generally requires paired cells for optimal performance
Best_for: 10x Multiome datasets; multimodal atlas projects; users already working in Seurat ecosystem
Study_examples: CITE-seq PBMC; 10x Multiome PBMC; many Human Cell Atlas studies
Benchmarked_in: scIB multimodal benchmarks; OpenProblems Multimodal; numerous RNA+ATAC benchmarking studies
Preprocessing_steps: Start from raw counts data, fragment file and peak-gene matrix and then process data and QC based on Seurat/Signac typicall steps
Input_formats: Seurat objects; h5Seurat; h5ad (via conversion); 10x Cell Ranger outputs; peak matrices
Input_details: RNA counts and ATAC peak accessibility from matched cells; supports LSI reduction for ATAC and PCA for RNA; batch metadata optional
Output_formats: WNN graph; UMAP; clustering; integrated embeddings; modality weights; gene activity scores
Underlying_method: Weighted Nearest Neighbor graph integration combining modality-specific neighbor graphs (PCA for RNA, LSI for ATAC)

Use_case: Probabilistic latent integration, denoising and batch correction of paired and unpaired single-cell modalities
Strengths: Scalable in cell numbers (> 1 million), probabilistic, handles batch effects, handles paired and unpaired data, strong scverse and svi-tools integration, widely benchmarked
Limitations: Requires model training, GPU strongly preferred for large datasets, latent dimensions not directly interpretable, ATAC peak-space choices matter
Study_examples: 10x Multiome plus separate RNA-only or ATAC-only datasets; multimodal atlas construction
Installation_source: pip install scvi-tools; conda/mamba via conda-forge
Preprocessing_steps: Start from raw counts data and peak-gene data, QC done as part of the process
Input_formats: MuData / AnnData / h5ad; RNA counts; ATAC peak counts or binary accessibility
Input_details: RNA genes and ATAC peaks; paired or unpaired cells; optional covariates such as donor/batch
Output_formats: Shared latent embedding; imputed modalities; normalized expression; accessibility estimates; DE/DA outputs

Use_case: Fast batch integration in PCA space
